_meta.name: golden-retrieval
_meta.criteria: Hand-labeled hold-out golden retrieval set; near-complete relevance labeling (each query judged against all 13 chunks); relevance = direct satisfaction of the information need, not topical overlap; never tuned against.

input.query: a delicate floral cup with jasmine and bright citrus

cases.notes: Distinctive product vocabulary maps to the Yirgacheffe single origin (dense nearest-neighbour).

input.query: smooth low-acid coffee for late evenings

cases.notes: Low-acid / late-evening need is satisfied only by the Midnight Decaf product.

input.query: kraft tones, natural light, texture over gloss

cases.notes: Exact visual-style phrasing — a sparse/FTS-leaning hit on the Visual Style playbook chunk.

input.query: the latest specialty coffee market trend

cases.notes: Two near-identical market-intel docs share the term; 'latest' must pick the fresher 2026 one.

input.query: what makes our single-origin coffee special and worth the price

cases.notes: Spans the Mission (why single-origin) and the Yirgacheffe product (what's special) — rewards re-ranking both above topical noise.

input.query: brand voice, tone, do and don't language, and product details

cases.notes: The Copywriting agent's real information need — Brand Voice + all three products.

input.query: brand visual style, imagery, colour palette, and product look

cases.notes: The Creative Director's real information need — Visual Style + all three products.

input.query: which past post drove the most engagement

cases.notes: Performance need: the Pour Over Sunday post has the higher engagement_rate of the two historical posts.

input.query: who are we talking to — our audience

cases.notes: Audience need maps to the Audience Persona playbook chunk.

input.query: how often and when should we post to the feed

cases.notes: Cadence/timing need maps to the Feed Cadence platform-guidance chunk.
